breaking_news.title: GlassWorm Supply Chain Attack Compromises Open VSX and GitHub Repositories
breaking_news.summary:
Threat actors are abusing Open VSX extensions and stolen GitHub tokens to inject malware into hundreds of Python repositories and developer tools. The campaign leverages dependency abuse to turn benign extensions into transitive delivery vehicles for the GlassWorm loader.

This supply chain compromise threatens millions of developers and organizations relying on compromised Python packages and VS Code extensions. The widespread nature of the attack highlights significant risks in the software development supply chain ecosystem.

breaking_news.title: Nine Critical AppArmor Vulnerabilities Expose 12 Million Linux Systems
breaking_news.summary:
Researchers disclosed nine critical vulnerabilities in the Linux AppArmor security module, collectively dubbed CrackArmor. These flaws allow unprivileged users to gain root access, break container isolation, and crash systems across millions of enterprise instances.

The vulnerabilities affect over 12 million enterprise Linux systems running Ubuntu, Debian, and SUSE distributions by default. This poses a severe risk to cloud platforms, Kubernetes clusters, and IoT environments relying on AppArmor for mandatory access control.

breaking_news.title: Medical Giant Stryker Wipes Tens of Thousands of Devices in Cyberattack
breaking_news.summary:
Medical technology giant Stryker confirmed a cyberattack that remotely wiped tens of thousands of employee devices within its internal Microsoft environment. The incident has caused significant disruption to digital ordering systems, though surgical tools remain safe for use.

The attack highlights the vulnerability of critical healthcare infrastructure to sophisticated cyber threats that can cripple operational capabilities without traditional malware. Hospitals and medical facilities relying on Stryker's digital systems face ongoing operational challenges during the recovery period.

breaking_news.title: ShinyHunters Claims One Petabyte Data Theft from Telus Digital
breaking_news.summary:
Ransomware group ShinyHunters claims to have stolen up to one petabyte of data from Canadian telecom giant Telus Digital. The breach allegedly includes support recordings, source code, and sensitive employee records following a significant compromise.

This massive data exfiltration represents one of the largest breaches in the telecommunications sector, exposing vast amounts of proprietary and personal information. The incident underscores the growing threat of data theft operations targeting major service providers.

breaking_news.title: Russia-Linked Laundry Bear APT Targets Ukraine with DRILLAPP Backdoor
breaking_news.summary:
Russia-linked APT group Laundry Bear is targeting Ukrainian organizations with a new DRILLAPP backdoor campaign. The threat actors utilize Microsoft Edge debugging features to evade detection while deploying spyware against defense and government entities.

This espionage operation demonstrates the evolving tactics of state-sponsored actors in the ongoing cyber conflict between Russia and Ukraine. The use of legitimate browser tools for stealthy persistence complicates detection and response efforts for targeted organizations.

breaking_news.title: UK Companies House Flaw Exposes Director Details and Company Records
breaking_news.summary:
The UK government agency Companies House confirmed a security flaw in its WebFiling service exposed confidential director details and company records. The vulnerability allowed unauthorized users to view sensitive information and potentially alter company records before the service was taken offline.

This breach compromises the integrity of the UK's corporate registry, affecting millions of businesses and their directors. The incident raises serious concerns about the security of critical government infrastructure managing national business data.

breaking_news.title: Storm-2561 Harvests Corporate Logins via Spoofed VPN Sites
breaking_news.summary:
Threat actor Storm-2561 is conducting a credential theft campaign using SEO-poisoned search results to distribute fake VPN clients. The attackers impersonate legitimate vendors like Ivanti, Cisco, and Fortinet to trick users into downloading trojanized installers.

Corporate login credentials are being harvested from victims who believe they are downloading secure enterprise VPN software. This campaign targets enterprise security infrastructure, potentially granting attackers access to sensitive internal networks and systems.

breaking_news.title: AWS Bedrock AI Code Interpreter Flaw Allows Data Leaks
breaking_news.summary:
Researchers identified a data leak risk in AWS Bedrock's AI Code Interpreter tool that allows sensitive cloud data exposure via DNS queries. The flaw exists within the AgentCore Code Interpreter sandbox, enabling unauthorized data exfiltration during AI operations.

Organizations utilizing AWS Bedrock for AI development face potential risks of sensitive information leakage through this vulnerability. The discovery highlights emerging security challenges associated with integrating AI agents into cloud infrastructure environments.
